title: Lake Pontchartrain Causeway - Wikipedia
content:
### Coordinate Information
The exact coordinates (degrees, minutes, seconds, and decimal) for the south and north ends of the bridge are not explicitly stated in the provided text.

### Terminus Locations
| Feature | Location | Parish |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Southern Terminus | Metairie, Louisiana | Jefferson Parish |
| Northern Terminus | Mandeville, Louisiana | St. Tammany Parish |

### Bridge Lengths and Measurements
| Description | Miles | Kilometers |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Longer of the two parallel bridges | 23.83 mi | 38.35 km |
| Original causeway span (1956) | 23.86 mi | 38.40 km |
| Parallel span length difference (1969) | 0.01 mi longer than original | 16 m longer than original |
| Length physical over water (US claim) | 23.79 mi | 38.28 km |
| Navigation channel bascule bridge location | 8 miles south of the north shore | 13 km south of the north shore |
| Total driving distance on continuous elevated roadway (Manchac/Bonnet Carré/LaBranche) | Over 38 mi | 61 km |

### Financial and Toll Data
| Year/Event | Type | Amount | Equivalent (2024 Dollars) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1956 | Construction Cost (Original) | $46 million | $410 million |
| 1969 | Construction Cost (Parallel) | $30 million | $200 million |
| Pre-1999 | Standard Toll (Each direction) | $1.50 | Not stated |
| May 1999 | Southbound Toll (North Shore collection) | $3.00 | Not stated |
| 2017–Present | Cash Toll (Southbound) | $5.00 | Not stated |
| 2017–Present | Toll Tag (Southbound) | $3.00 | Not stated |
| Pre-2017 | Toll Tag (Southbound) | $2.00 | Not stated |

### Structural and Historical Specifications
*   **Bridge Type:** Fixed link composed of two parallel bridges; trestle bridges; bascule bridges.
*   **Support Structure:** 9,500 concrete pilings.
*   **Opening Dates:**
    *   Original span: 1956.
    *   Parallel span: May 10, 1969.
*   **Guinness World Record Categories:**
    *   Longest bridge over water (continuous).
    *   Formerly: Longest bridge over water in the world.
*   **Key Figures:**
    *   Bernard de Marigny: Mandeville founder, early ferry service (early 19th century – mid-1930s).
    *   Ernest M. Loëb Jr.: Envisioned the modern causeway project (1948).
    *   Ernest M. Loëb III: President of Ernest M. Loëb & Company, project planner.
    *   James E. Walters Sr.: Project director for Louisiana Bridge Company.
*   **Safety and Incidents:**
    *   June 16, 1964: Barge collision; six deaths; bus plunged into the lake.
    *   August 29, 2005: Hurricane Katrina; damage limited to turnarounds; 17 spans lost on the I-10 Twin Span (not the Causeway).
    *   September 19, 2005: Reopened to public after Hurricane Katrina.
*   **Designations:** National Historic Civil Engineering Landmark (designated 2013 by the American Society of Civil Engineers).

### Comparative Infrastructure in Louisiana
Other highway spans with a total length of 5 miles (8.0 km) or more:
1.  Manchac Swamp Bridge (I-55)
2.  Atchafalaya Basin Bridge (I-10)
3.  Louisiana Highway 1 Bridge
4.  Bonnet Carré Spillway Bridge (I-10)
5.  Chacahoula Swamp Bridge (US 90)
6.  Lake Pontchartrain Twin Spans (I-10)
7.  LaBranche Wetlands Bridge (I-310)
8.  Norfolk Southern Lake Pontchartrain Bridge (Railway, 5.8 miles/9.3 km)

*Note: The Maestri Bridge (US 11) is approximately 4.8 miles (7.7 km) long.*
